what_it_does: Creates a portrait composed decision-card page for the decision id "merge-sa-authorize" by loading shared CSS and runtime scripts, fetching layout and decision JSON, rendering a DNA decision archetype in pending state, wrapping it in an explained slide, and mounting it into the page.
observations: Defines an empty #mount container with min-height:100vh and container-type:inline-size.
Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
Loads runtime scripts for surface, organisms, library covers, decision rendering, and archetypes.
Uses a fixed ID value of "merge-sa-authorize" to find one record in /content/decisions.json.
Builds a decision://global/merge-sa-authorize address and injects it into the rendered identity.
Forces the rendered identity state and resolver state to "pending".
Uses rec.legibility.why as the explanation_source resolver value when present.
Adds class "dv-c" to the rendered explained view before appending it.
standouts: The file is a thin runtime composition shell around a single decision id.
The rendered output is produced through DNA.renderArchetype followed by DNA.renderExplained.
The inline comment frames the page as a composed decision view containing both interface and slide-telling.
conventions: Uses a minimal HTML document with viewport metadata and external shared assets.
Keeps decision content data-driven by fetching JSON rather than embedding the decision body in HTML.
Uses an async IIFE for page initialization.
Relies on a global DNA runtime object supplied by script tags.
Uses CSS custom properties and container queries through #mount container setup.
concerns: No local handling for failed fetches, missing JSON fields, or a missing decision record.
The code assumes layouts.archetypes["decision-card"] exists before rendering.
The code passes rec into DNA.decisionDevice and Object.assign without guarding against an undefined record.
External font loading can affect rendering if the network request fails or is blocked.
notes: This file is a single-id decision-card page that delegates nearly all structure and content to shared DNA runtime data and renderers.
questions: What should the page show if "merge-sa-authorize" is not present in decisions.json?
Is "pending" always the intended state for this rendered decision view?
What visual or behavioral changes are controlled by composed:true and visualDevice in this context?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file composes a decision interface into an explained slide view using shared runtime renderers.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make one decision record displayable as a reusable composed presentation surface without hardcoding the decision content into the HTML.
novelty: The page treats a decision as an addressable rendered archetype and then wraps that archetype in an explanatory slide layer.
contribution: Contributes a file-local pattern for binding one decision id to shared layout/data JSON, generating an addressable pending-state decision view, and presenting it through the composed decision slide renderer.
summary_for_embedding: HTML shell for the merge-sa-authorize decision-card view that fetches shared layout and decision data, renders a pending DNA decision archetype, wraps it in an explained slide, and mounts it to the page.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting a specific decision as a composed, data-driven decision interface with explanatory slide context.
